name: File Deletion
description: Refers to events where files are removed from a system or storage device. These events can indicate legitimate housekeeping activities or malicious actions such as attackers attempting to cover their tracks. Monitoring file deletions helps organizations identify unauthorized or suspicious activities.

sysmon.description: Logs file deletion events, including details such as file paths and responsible processes.

powershell_monitoring.description: Filter Security logs for deletion-specific Object Access events.

audit_framework.configuration_rule: auditctl -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S unlink -S rename -S rmdir -k file_deletion

endpoint_security_framework.description: Monitor events to capture file deletion activities.

file_system_events.description: Track file deletion activities in real-time.

siem_integration.action: Forward file deletion logs to a SIEM for centralized monitoring and correlation with other events.

log_sources.Name: auditd:SYSCALL
log_sources.Channel: unlink/unlinkat on service binaries or data targets

log_sources.Name: macos:osquery
log_sources.Channel: CREATE, DELETE, WRITE: Stored data manipulation attempts by unauthorized processes

log_sources.Name: WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Backup
log_sources.Channel: Windows Backup Catalog deletion or catalog corruption

log_sources.Name: ics:fw_app
log_sources.Channel: Router Flask UI clears Suricata rule file or firewall state (log cleared)

log_sources.Name: ics:plc_app
log_sources.Channel: OpenPLC /remove-program endpoint deleting uploaded program files

log_sources.Name: ics:openplc_debug
log_sources.Channel: OpenPLC debug log showing program file deletion
